2013-12-01.title: Politics and a Ruptured Tendon Don’t Faze Lead Iran Negotiator
2013-12-01.abstract: The focus of Wendy Sherman over the past two years helped prepare the ground for talks with Iran that gained momentum, both in public and through a secret channel.

2013-12-01.title: A Rustic Paradise, Open for Development
2013-12-01.abstract: New zoning rules will open northwestern Mexico’s agricultural heartland to denser housing and golf courses, but there is concern that the Guadalupe Valley cannot sustain this intensified development.

2013-12-01.title: Thai Protests Turn Volatile as at Least 3 Are Shot Dead
2013-12-01.abstract: At least three deaths, and the demonstrators’ increasingly provocative moves, raised fears that the unrest could spread to other parts of Bangkok.

2013-12-01.title: China Launches Moon Rover Mission
2013-12-01.abstract: If the mission succeeds, China will be the third country to achieve a “soft” landing on the moon, one that allows a craft to operate after descending.

2013-12-01.title: Thousands Demand Resignation of Ukraine Leader
2013-12-01.abstract: The largest showing of fury in 11 days over President Viktor F. Yanukovich’s refusal to sign political and trade accords with the European Union came as signs of fissure emerged within the government.

2013-12-02.title: Ontario Man Accused of Plot to Sell Secrets
2013-12-02.abstract: Qing Quentin Huang was allegedly trying to sell secrets about Canada’s new naval shipbuilding program to China, the Royal Canadian Mounted Police said.

2013-12-02.title: Israel’s Foreign Minister Returns, but Abrasive Style Appears Absent
2013-12-02.abstract: Newly acquitted of fraud charges, Avigdor Lieberman returned to his post as Israel’s foreign minister, but his abrasive political style has not followed suit.

2013-12-02.title: In Egypt Charter, New Rights, but No Great Change
2013-12-02.abstract: Experts say the draft constitution gives privileged status to institutions that have repeatedly thwarted change during Egypt’s years of revolutionary turmoil.

2013-12-02.title: In the East China Sea, a Far Bigger Test of Power Looms
2013-12-02.abstract: In an era when the United States has been focused on new forms of conflict, the dangerous contest suddenly erupting in the East China Sea seems almost like a throwback to the Cold War.

2013-12-02.title: Natalya Gorbanevskaya, Soviet Dissident and Poet, Dies at 77
2013-12-02.abstract: Ms. Gorbanevskaya was arrested after protesting the Soviet Union’s 1968 invasion of Czechoslovakia, and later confined to a psychiatric hospital for her writings.

2013-12-03.title: Croatian Government to Pursue Law Allowing Civil Unions for Gay Couples
2013-12-03.abstract: After a majority of Croats voted to effectively ban same-sex marriages, Prime Minister Zoran Milanovic’s government pledged Monday to pursue a law allowing civil partnerships.

2013-12-03.title: Mexico: U.S. Indicts a Former Governor
2013-12-03.abstract: The United States has indicted Tomás Yarrington Ruvalcaba, a former governor of a Mexican state and a member of the Institutional Revolutionary Party, on racketeering and money laundering charges.

2013-12-03.title: Britain: Envoy Aims to Mend Iran Ties
2013-12-03.abstract: Britain’s new envoy to Tehran will travel to Iran on Tuesday, the first diplomatic visit by a British official since Britain’s embassy in Tehran was stormed more than two years ago.

2013-12-03.title: U.S. Pledges New Funds to Fight AIDS
2013-12-03.abstract: The United States will commit up to $5 billion to the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria, President Obama said Monday at the White House.

2013-12-03.title: Kurds’ Oil Deals With Turkey Raise Fears of Fissures in Iraq
2013-12-03.abstract: Iraqi Kurds are selling oil and natural gas directly to Turkey, upsetting Baghdad and Washington, which fear a broader independence for Kurds in Iraq’s north.

2013-12-04.title: Greenpeace Activists Detail Russia’s Capricious Justice System
2013-12-04.abstract: The crew of a Greenpeace ship seized by Russian authorities in the Arctic in September described a prosecution bound by the letter of the law but remarkably opaque.

2013-12-04.title: Tests on Arafat Find No Proof of Radiation Poisoning
2013-12-04.abstract: French investigators’ conclusion partly contradicted and partly confirmed those of Swiss scientists who found abnormal polonium-210 levels in the remains of Yasir Arafat.

2013-12-04.title: In Kiev City Hall, Energized Protesters Settle In for a Long Winter’s Uprising
2013-12-04.abstract: Protesters have taken over the first two floors of City Hall in the Ukrainian capital and are taking unusual measures to assure they will not be removed easily.

2013-12-04.title: Germany: Ban Sought on Political Party
2013-12-04.abstract: Germany’s 16 states submitted a petition to the Constitutional Court on Tuesday to ban the far-right National Democratic Party on the grounds that its ideology resembles that of the Nazis.

2013-12-04.title: $12 Billion Is Pledged to Fight AIDS, TB and Malaria
2013-12-04.abstract: The amount pledged by donor countries over three years to a global fund was more than the amount raised in 2010, but far short of the goal.

2013-12-05.title: Iran Takes Charm Offensive to the Persian Gulf
2013-12-05.abstract: Iran’s foreign minister, Mohammad Javad Zarif, is touring the region, trying to undo years of tensions with Arab neighbors, which harbor deep suspicions of Tehran.

2013-12-05.title: Amsterdam Has a Deal for Alcoholics: Work Paid in Beer
2013-12-05.abstract: Under a government-funded program denounced by conservative politicians, the city of tolerance is giving alcoholics street-cleaning jobs and paying them partly in beer.

2013-12-05.title: Kerry Vows to Help Moldova Hew to the West
2013-12-05.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry’s pledge was a measure of encouragement for a government that is trying to maintain public support for its outreach to the West.

2013-12-05.title: U.N. Official Details Plans for Removing Syria’s Chemical Arms
2013-12-05.abstract: The official said the most dangerous materials would be sealed and sent to the Mediterranean port of Latakia, where ships would take them to a second foreign port.

2013-12-05.title: Gang Members Said to Consider Blackmailing Toronto’s Mayor
2013-12-05.abstract: Interviews and wiretaps suggested that Mayor Rob Ford regularly used drugs, the Toronto police wrote, and that dealers discussed blackmailing him.

2013-12-06.title: In Guyana, Feeling Stifled After Needling Government in Song
2013-12-06.abstract: A conflict has surfaced between the Guyanese government and calypso singers, who accuse it of repressing their often politically charged music by keeping it off the airwaves.

2013-12-06.title: Scenes from Kiev’s Independence Square
2013-12-06.abstract: Demonstrators have been occupying Independence Square since last Sunday.

2013-12-06.title: Somalia: Suicide Bomber Attacks Convoy in the North
2013-12-06.abstract: A suicide car bomber struck a security force’s convoy on Thursday in northern Somalia, killing at least seven people and wounding 37.

2013-12-06.title: Obama’s Path Was Shaped by Mandela’s Story
2013-12-06.abstract: President Obama recalled that his public career began at an anti-apartheid rally in 1979, imbued with the hope Nelson Mandela had generated.

2013-12-06.title: New Emotion, Hope, Sweeps Across Iran in Aftermath of Temporary Nuclear Pact
2013-12-06.abstract: The promise of easing some sanctions has brought optimism and the possibility of new business ventures.

2013-12-07.title: Front Pages Mark Mandela’s Death
2013-12-07.abstract: Newspapers around the world mourned the death of Nelson Mandela.

2013-12-07.title: Rising Tension Seen in North Korea and at Sea Border
2013-12-07.abstract: South Korea’s intelligence chief said the North had increased weapons near a disputed border, in what analysts said might be an attempt to divert attention from an internal power struggle.

2013-12-07.title: Japan’s Parliament Approves a Secrecy Law Amid Protests
2013-12-07.abstract: Prime Minister Shinzo Abe said the new law would strengthen national security, but opponents warned it would stifle democracy.

2013-12-07.title: Decades After the Cultural Revolution, a Rare Letter of Remorse
2013-12-07.abstract: The son of a famed Chinese official posted a letter apologizing for his role in the abuse of his teachers during the Cultural Revolution.

2013-12-07.title: With Protests at Home, Ukraine’s Leader Leaves China Without Aid Deal
2013-12-07.abstract: Viktor F. Yanukovich is desperate for cash to meet gas bills and debt payments, but his state visit showed how circumspect China has been in the tug-of-war over Ukraine between Russia and the European Union.

2013-12-08.title: In an Israeli Plan, Bedouins See a Threat to Their Way of Life
2013-12-08.abstract: After decades of discrimination and neglect, many Bedouins and their supporters see, in Israeli offers of jobs, education and homes, a plot to destroy their way of life.

2013-12-08.title: Musicals Couldn’t Be Hotter Off Broadway (by 7,000 Miles)
2013-12-08.abstract: Seoul, South Korea, has become a boomtown for American musicals, with producers opening Korean-language versions of hits and flops alike.

2013-12-08.title: In Nation Remade by Mandela, Social Equality Remains Elusive
2013-12-08.abstract: Black South Africans are still very far behind whites when it comes to income and education, and by some measures falling further back.

2013-12-08.title: Congress Party Defeated in Indian State Elections
2013-12-08.abstract: The results signaled the waning power of the dynasty that has dominated Indian politics for nearly all the post-Independence era.

2013-12-08.title: In Soweto and Beyond, Mandela Still Serves as a Beacon of Hope
2013-12-08.abstract: South Africans across the country began a week of commemorations of Nelson Mandela’s life with what officials called a day of prayer and reflection.

2013-12-09.title: Afghan Leader Agrees to Talks on Closer Iran Ties
2013-12-09.abstract: President Hamid Karzai’s meeting with President Hassan Rouhani of Iran also helped distance him from the United States, with which he has had sharp disagreements.

2013-12-09.title: Venezuelan Mayoral Votes Show No Big Power Shift
2013-12-09.abstract: Hundreds of mayoral contests in Venezuela on Sunday are seen as an early test of President Nicolás Maduro and his ability to carry the country further along a socialist path.

2013-12-09.title: A Soweto Church Says Goodbye to Mandela
2013-12-09.abstract: At Regina Mundi Catholic Church in Soweto, not far from his former home, parishioners gathered on Sunday to celebrate the memories and legacy of Nelson Mandela.

2013-12-09.title: Eyes on Iran, Navy in Gulf Stays at Ready
2013-12-09.abstract: With a temporary nuclear agreement reached, the American Navy’s presence in the gulf is part of a plan to maintain military pressure while giving diplomacy a chance to work.

2013-12-09.title: Amid Rows of Tin Shacks, a Luxury Hotel Rises
2013-12-09.abstract: In the Kliptown section of Soweto, $180-a-night hotel rooms sit 30 yards from tin shacks.

2013-12-10.title: After Mandela, a Test at the Polls for His Party
2013-12-10.abstract: After the death of its most beloved leader, the African National Congress could face its most competitive elections since it came to power in 1994.

2013-12-10.title: Argentina: Looting Spreads as Police Officers Stage Strikes
2013-12-10.abstract: Police officers in 14 of the country’s 23 provinces have staged work stoppages in recent days, local news media said.

2013-12-10.title: Protests Continue in Downtown Kiev
2013-12-10.abstract: Tens of thousands were gathered at Independence Square in Kiev for antigovernment protests on Sunday night.

2013-12-10.title: Stopping Bloodshed in the Central African Republic Amid Ghosts of Genocide
2013-12-10.abstract: United Nations diplomats search for a plan for keeping the nation from becoming another Rwanda or Bosnia.

2013-12-10.title: Ukrainian Protesters Struggle to Keep Police and Cold at Bay
2013-12-10.abstract: A confrontation at a tiny camp of Ukrainian protesters was an example of the seesaw struggle with the police for control of strategic areas in Kiev.

2013-12-11.title: Honoring Mandela
2013-12-11.abstract: Nelson Mandela’s death spurred an international outpouring of praise, remembrance and celebration.

2013-12-11.title: Uruguay Acts to Legalize Marijuana
2013-12-11.abstract: Supporters said the move would remove the marijuana trade from the domain of illegal traffickers, allowing the authorities to regulate its consumption.

2013-12-11.title: Impasse Over Security Deal Adds to Afghan Economy’s Troubles
2013-12-11.abstract: The business climate has worsened since President Hamid Karzai delayed his signing of the security deal, with rising food and fuel prices and a slump in lending and investment.

2013-12-11.title: Iraq: 13 Sunnis Killed, Then 12 Shiites
2013-12-11.abstract: Seven Sunni construction workers were killed by unidentified gunmen who fired on them from a speeding S.U.V. in Abara, a majority-Sunni village in Diyala Province, police and medical officials said.

2013-12-11.title: Israel: Law Reduces Migrant Detention
2013-12-11.abstract: The Israeli Parliament passed legislation on Tuesday allowing for the detention of African migrants and asylum seekers who enter the country illegally for up to a year without trial.

2013-12-12.title: Thai Prime Minister Facing Leadership Questions Old and New
2013-12-12.abstract: Two and a half years after taking office, Yingluck Shinawatra is battling many of the same perceptions held of her in high school.

2013-12-12.title: Development Blitz Provokes a Murmur of Dissent in Monaco
2013-12-12.abstract: Under Prince Albert II, the principality has seen a rush of construction as old landmarks give way to opulent high-rises that has left some residents unhappy, but helpless.

2013-12-12.title: Canada to Stop Delivering Mail to City Homes Over 5 Years
2013-12-12.abstract: Canada’s postal service said Wednesday that it would cease home delivery over the next five years, and substantially increase postal rates.

2013-12-12.title: As Winter Nears, Chinese Capital Is Blanketed in Green
2013-12-12.abstract: In a city crowded with BMWs, upscale malls and produce-packed supermarkets, the stockpiling of cabbage speaks to an era of scarcity that still haunts Chinese of a certain age.

2013-12-12.title: Hometown Remembers Madiba the Villager, Well Before Mandela the Icon
2013-12-12.abstract: While the world admires Nelson Mandela as a freedom fighter, residents in his hometown, where he will be buried on Sunday, considered him just another villager.

2013-12-13.title: Kosovo Attains Status (on Facebook) It Has Sought for Years: Nation
2013-12-13.abstract: Kosovars can now register Kosovo as their home country on Facebook. Their leaders fought hard for what they see as a validation of sovereignty.

2013-12-13.title: Chemical Arms Used Repeatedly in Syria, U.N. Says
2013-12-13.abstract: Chemical weapons were used repeatedly in the Syria conflict this year, not only in a well-documented Aug. 21 attack but also in four other instances, a United Nations panels said.

2013-12-13.title: Belgian Senate Votes to Allow Euthanasia for Terminally Ill Children
2013-12-13.abstract: Belgium took a big step toward allowing euthanasia for terminally ill children, after its Senate voted to extend to minors a 2002 law legalizing the practice for adults.

2013-12-13.title: Somalia: From Canada, a Candidate
2013-12-13.abstract: President Hassan Sheik Mohamud nominated a Somali-Canadian as the country’s new prime minister after the previous prime minister was removed by Parliament in a no-confidence vote.

2013-12-13.title: Spain: Catalonia Calls Vote on Split
2013-12-13.abstract: Catalonia, Spain’s economic powerhouse, headed toward a standoff with Madrid on Thursday after separatist parties in the region announced a date next year for an independence referendum.

2013-12-14.title: U.N. Chief Responds to Syria Report
2013-12-14.abstract: The United Nations secretary general on Friday said that those who used chemical weapons in Syria should be brought to justice.

2013-12-14.title: Squatters in the Philippines Face Obstacles in Their Rebuilding Efforts
2013-12-14.abstract: Following Typhoon Haiyan, squatters face a second evection.

2013-12-14.title: Photographer's Journal: Jes Aznar
2013-12-14.abstract: Covering the aftermath of Typhoon Haiyan in the Philippines was an emotional experience for Jes Aznar, who himself is Filipino.

2013-12-14.title: Photographer's Journal: Sergey Ponomarev
2013-12-14.abstract: Covering the aftermath of Typhoon Haiyan was a challenging assignment in many ways. Sergey Ponomarev, a freelance photographer, reflects on his experience.

2013-12-14.title: Three Photographers Reflect on Typhoon Haiyan
2013-12-14.abstract: More than a month after Hayian ended 6,000 lives, the Philippine people are still recovering. In these videos, New York Times photographers recount their experiences covering the disaster.

2013-12-15.title: Egypt to Vote Next Month on New Constitution
2013-12-15.abstract: It will be the first gauge of support for the military’s ouster of Egypt’s first fairly elected president, Mohamed Morsi of the Muslim Brotherhood.

2013-12-15.title: A Violent Border City’s Revival
2013-12-15.abstract: In Ciudad Juárez, a symbol of drug war devastation, the killing has subsided, replaced by new businesses, a nightlife and hope.

2013-12-15.title: Ciudad Juárez, a Border City Known for Killing, Gets Back to Living
2013-12-15.abstract: Only a few years ago, Ciudad Juárez was a symbol of drug war devastation. But the killing has subsided, replaced by new businesses, returning residents and cautious hope.

2013-12-15.title: Far Right in Eastern Europe Makes Gains as Syrians Arrive
2013-12-15.abstract: A party known as Ataka, which means attack, champions “Bulgaria for Bulgarians” and has denounced Syrian refugees as terrorists whom Bulgaria, the European Union’s poorest nation, must expel.

2013-12-15.title: Following the Murky Trail Left by Diamonds and Cash
2013-12-15.abstract: A flurry of legal cases, investigations and leaked bank documents have drawn attention to the opaque movement of diamond-backed money.

2013-12-16.title: Ukrainians Gear Up for Holidays as Protests Continue
2013-12-16.abstract: Though Kiev, the capital, has been fixated on political demonstrations for more than three weeks, it is still holiday season, and for most of the city, life goes on.

2013-12-16.title: Gaza, Vexed by Floods, Gets Fuel and Power
2013-12-16.abstract: Gaza’s lone power plant sputtered back to life for the first time in seven weeks after Qatar paid for fuel shipped from Israel.

2013-12-16.title: As Merkel Embarks on Third Term, Critics Urge Her to Be Daring
2013-12-16.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany’s legacy could be in Europe, nurturing its fragile economic recovery and encouraging further European integration.

2013-12-16.title: Egyptian Christians Bridle at Prison Terms for Copts Only in Fatal Clash
2013-12-16.abstract: With no Muslims prosecuted in at least five killings in the violence, the military-backed government was accused of breaking a promise to curb bias.

2013-12-16.title: Lawmakers Fault BBC Leaders Over Severance Pay
2013-12-16.abstract: A report by an oversight committee of the British Parliament said that “cronyism” at a “dysfunctional” BBC led to executives getting larger payoffs than they deserved.

2013-12-17.title: India: Ex-Official Released on Bail
2013-12-17.abstract: Lalu Prasad, a once-powerful political leader from northern India, emerged from jail on Monday after spending more than two months behind bars on corruption charges.

2013-12-17.title: Russia: 4 Die in North Caucasus Raid
2013-12-17.abstract: Members of Russia’s security services killed four men suspected of being militants when they stormed an apartment building after a tense overnight standoff in the North Caucasus region.

2013-12-17.title: Aid Challenge in Syria Mounts for U.N. Official
2013-12-17.abstract: Some rights advocates say Valerie Amos, the United Nations relief coordinator, has been too patient with the Syrian government in pushing for access to areas in need.

2013-12-17.title: E.U. Pledges Aid for Mideast Peace
2013-12-17.abstract: The European Union will provide “an unprecedented package of European political, economic and security support” to the Israelis and Palestinians if they reach a permanent peace deal, the union said in a statement.

2013-12-17.title: Pope Replaces Conservative U.S. Cardinal on Influential Vatican Committee
2013-12-17.abstract: Pope Francis, in naming bishops to a powerful Vatican committee, replaced a conservative American cardinal with another American considered more of a moderate on social issues.

2013-12-18.title: Graft Inquiry Intensifies Turkish Political Rivalry
2013-12-18.abstract: A corruption inquiry connected to the construction business and public financing of real estate projects poses a potential threat to the Turkish prime minister.

2013-12-18.title: India’s Upper House Approves Anticorruption Agency
2013-12-18.abstract: Ending four decades of debate over how to curb corruption, India’s upper house of Parliament approved the creation of an independent agency to fight bribery and other illegal acts.

2013-12-18.title: U.S. Military Deaths in Afghanistan
2013-12-18.abstract: The Department of Defense has identified 2,278 American service members who have died as a part of the Afghan war and related operations.

2013-12-18.title: Three Days of Violence in South Sudan Leave Scores Dead
2013-12-18.abstract: At least 75 people were killed, the South Sudanese interior minister said, but Reuters put the figure at at least 400 dead and up to 800 wounded.

2013-12-18.title: A Top Iraqi Official’s Advice to Karzai? Take America’s Deal
2013-12-18.abstract: With the benefit of hindsight, the Iraqi foreign minister, Hoshyar Zebari, urged President Hamid Karzai of Afghanistan to get over his differences with the Americans and sign a security agreement with the United States.

2013-12-19.title: Egypt Accuses Morsi of Vast Terrorist Plot
2013-12-19.abstract: As the military-backed government filed new criminal charges against Mohamed Morsi, the deposed president, human rights groups called the allegations preposterous.

2013-12-19.title: Eyad El-Sarraj, Psychiatrist Who Fought for Palestinians’ Rights, Dies at 70
2013-12-19.abstract: Dr. Sarraj pioneered mental health care in Gaza and became an internationally recognized human rights advocate, criticizing both the Israeli and Palestinian authorities.

2013-12-19.title: The Ambassador of Spanish Ham
2013-12-19.abstract: Florencio Sanchidrián is said to be one of the world’s great cortadors de jamon, or Iberian ham cutters. Cutting ham is not just a profession, but an art, and Mr. Sanchidrián shows how it is done.

2013-12-19.title: Spreading Spain’s Glory in Thin, Slow Slices
2013-12-19.abstract: Florencio Sanchidrián, who says he considers himself an ambassador for Spain’s famed Ibérico ham, travels the world slicing meat, with flair, for V.I.P.’s.

2013-12-19.title: Turkey Jailing the Most Journalists
2013-12-19.abstract: For the second consecutive year, Turkey is imprisoning more journalists than any other country, according to a report from the Committee to Protect Journalists released Wednesday.

2013-12-20.title: Hagel Criticizes Chinese Navy, Citing Near Miss
2013-12-20.abstract: Defense Secretary Chuck Hagel said the Chinese Navy acted irresponsibly in allowing a warship to pass perilously close to an American warship.

2013-12-20.title: Part of Ceiling Collapses During Sold-Out Show at London Theater
2013-12-20.abstract: At least seven people were seriously hurt and another 81 lightly injured, according to fire and medical officials, when the ceiling of a tall Edwardian theater collapsed Thursday night during a sold-out performance.

2013-12-20.title: Authorities Move Against Rights Group in Egypt
2013-12-20.abstract: The action against the Egyptian Center for Economic and Social Rights was a sign that the military-backed government has expanded its crackdown beyond the Islamist supporters of the deposed president, Mohamed Morsi.

2013-12-20.title: Defying Winter’s Dreariness With an Arctic Embrace
2013-12-20.abstract: In Britain, a growing number of people are defying the cold and wet by getting colder and wetter. Winter swimming’s popularity has risen.

2013-12-20.title: Growing Corruption Inquiry Hits Close to Turkish Leader
2013-12-20.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan weathered a summer of protest, but a corruption scandal poses a greater challenge.

2013-12-21.title: Leading the Charge Against Spain’s Mortgage Crisis
2013-12-21.abstract: Ada Colau’s group, the Platform for People Affected by Mortgages, has blocked hundreds of evictions and pressed for changes in Spain’s hard-line mortgage laws.

2013-12-21.title: Israel: Palestinian Killed Near Border
2013-12-21.abstract: Israeli forces fatally shot a Palestinian man who approached the border fence separating Gaza from Israel and wounded five others at several points along the fence, Palestinian medical officials said.

2013-12-21.title: China: Tibetan Sets Himself on Fire
2013-12-21.abstract: A monk in a Tibetan region of western China died after setting fire to himself on Thursday afternoon, according to two Tibet advocacy groups based outside China.

2013-12-21.title: Libya Militias Fleeing Cities, Leaving Chaos
2013-12-21.abstract: Civilian protesters and armed groups have chased militias from Tripoli and Benghazi for the first time since the ouster of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi in 2011.

2013-12-21.title: Canadian Court Strikes Down Laws on Sex Trade
2013-12-21.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled unanimously that three laws violated prostitutes’ constitutional right to “security of the person.”

2013-12-22.title: Michiaki Takahashi, 85, Who Tamed Chickenpox, Dies
2013-12-22.abstract: Dr. Takahashi, a researcher of measles and polio viruses, was spurred to action when his 3-year-old son fell ill.

2013-12-22.title: Antigovernment Protesters Vow to Block Elections in Thailand
2013-12-22.abstract: Demonstrators in Bangkok vowed to disrupt elections scheduled for Feb. 2 in their campaign to rid Thailand of its most influential political family.

2013-12-22.title: Free to Speak About Russia, but Now From a Safe Distance
2013-12-22.abstract: Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky, a former billionaire and a critic of President Vladimir V. Putin’s who was imprisoned for a decade, said in Berlin that he would work to help other political prisoners.

2013-12-22.title: In Blow to Leadership of ’11 Revolt, Egypt Activists Are Given 3 Years in Prison
2013-12-22.abstract: The convictions of three activists who played central roles in the uprising against former President Hosni Mubarak were seen as part of an effort to curb dissent.

2013-12-22.title: Bangladeshi Factory Owners Charged in Fire That Killed 112
2013-12-22.abstract: The case that came to symbolize the appalling working conditions in the textile industry is the first in which the authorities have sought to prosecute factory owners in the garment industry.

2013-12-23.title: Dozens Are Killed in Syrian Violence, Even Amid Preparations for Peace Talks
2013-12-23.abstract: At least 25 people were killed in Aleppo, antigovernment activists reported, while a suicide car bombing killed at least 10 people in the central province of Homs.

2013-12-23.title: Americans Evacuated From South Sudan
2013-12-23.abstract: Americans were flown out of a besieged United Nations compound, a day after a first attempt was fired upon.

2013-12-23.title: Partying Until Drunk and Disorderly in Britain
2013-12-23.abstract: British officials are considering solutions as large numbers of British youths go out on the weekends to get thoroughly, blindingly and often violently drunk.

2013-12-23.title: Q. and A. With Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky
2013-12-23.abstract: A group of Russian-speaking journalists on Sunday interviewed Mr. Khodorkovsky, newly freed after a decade of being a political prisoner.

2013-12-23.title: Clothing the American Government’s Employees
2013-12-23.abstract: In many overseas factories producing garments for the United States government, conditions are harsh and pay is low.

2013-12-24.title: Growing Mistrust Between U.S. and Turkey Is Played Out in Public
2013-12-24.abstract: After several years of close ties, the United States and Turkey find themselves at odds on a number of issues.

2013-12-24.title: British Premier Faces Growing Criticism Over a Push to Curb Immigration
2013-12-24.abstract: Fractures within Britain’s governing coalition add to the pressure on Prime Minister David Cameron as debate over limits on European Union immigrants intensifies.

2013-12-24.title: Archbishop and Imam Are United Across Battle Lines in Central African Republic
2013-12-24.abstract: Two religious leaders joined forces to try to head off brewing sectarian tensions in Central African Republic, where a brutal conflict between Christians and Muslims has killed hundreds this month alone.

2013-12-24.title: Alan Turing, Enigma Code-Breaker and Computer Pioneer, Wins Royal Pardon
2013-12-24.abstract: Alan Turing, a British mathematician, was convicted on charges of homosexuality in 1952; he committed suicide two years later at the age of 41.

2013-12-24.title: In China, Dreaming of a Less Smoggy Christmas
2013-12-24.abstract: Though Christmas Eve was an ordinary working day for most Chinese, there was plenty of irritation about the heavy air pollution that hit at least 78 cities.

2013-12-25.title: In Germany’s Rust Belt, a Polished but Ailing City
2013-12-25.abstract: Oberhausen, crowned “Germany’s Detroit” because it has the country’s highest per capita debt, is long past its industrial glory days, but there are limits to how far the city will go to pare its cultural institutions.

2013-12-25.title: Brazil: Floods and Landslides Kill 30
2013-12-25.abstract: Heavy rains in Espírito Santo and Minas Gerais, two states in southeastern Brazil, have left at least 30 people dead and forced about 50,000 people from their homes.

2013-12-25.title: A New Jolt to Filipino Siblings Orphaned by a Typhoon
2013-12-25.abstract: The disappearance of five siblings from a Tacloban evacuation center raised alarms about human trafficking, but they surfaced in an orphanage, reflecting a confused child protection system.

2013-12-25.title: Freed Russian Applies for Swiss Visa Allowing Travel in 26 Nations
2013-12-25.abstract: Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky, a former oil tycoon and political opponent of President Vladimir V. Putin, has submitted his application to Switzerland for a three-month visa.

2013-12-25.title: Rockets Fired by Taliban Hit U.S. Embassy in Kabul
2013-12-25.abstract: The Taliban claimed responsibility for the attack, which caused no casualties. Afghan officials said two other rockets hit elsewhere in Kabul, hurting three policemen.

2013-12-26.title: Rescuing a Predator
2013-12-26.abstract: The largest crocodile in South America is on the verge of extinction, and government and private efforts to rescue the species are having limited success.

2013-12-26.title: Venezuela’s Fitful Effort to Save a Scaly Predator
2013-12-26.abstract: A ranch that was expropriated by Venezuela’s government in 2009 represents both the hopes and the frustrations of conservationists who have worked to save the Orinoco crocodile.

2013-12-26.title: Highlights from Pope's Christmas Message
2013-12-26.abstract: Pope Francis delivered his first Christmas message from the Vatican.

2013-12-26.title: U.S. Sends Arms to Aid Iraq Fight With Extremists
2013-12-26.abstract: After an appeal for help, the United States is rushing dozens of missiles and surveillance drones to help Iraqi forces combat violence by a Qaeda-backed insurgency.

2013-12-26.title: Japanese Premier Visits Contentious War Shrine
2013-12-26.abstract: The visit by Prime Minister Shinzo Abe to the Yasukuni Shrine revived a practice that has long strained relations between Japan and its Asian neighbors.

2013-12-27.title: Israeli Jets Strike Gaza Strip in Response to Rocket Attacks
2013-12-27.abstract: The actions, the second in three days, continue a new wave of violence that has complicated peace talks.

2013-12-27.title: Ukraine: Third Suspect Detained in Beating of Activist
2013-12-27.abstract: Ukrainian authorities on Thursday increased the severity of potential charges in the case after initially deeming it a matter of “hooliganism.”

2013-12-27.title: South Africans Band Together, Finding a New Enemy in Highway Tolls
2013-12-27.abstract: A new road toll system has prompted a battle of words between protesters and the South African government.

2013-12-27.title: Frederick Fox, Milliner to Royals, Dies at 82
2013-12-27.abstract: Beginning in 1970, Mr. Fox designed more than 350 hats for Queen Elizabeth. His trademark skill, admirers said, was to mix whimsy and propriety.

2013-12-27.title: Deal to Move Okinawa Base Wins Approval
2013-12-27.abstract: A long-simmering dispute between the United States and Japan over the fate of a Marine base on Okinawa seemed to have been resolved on Friday.

2013-12-28.title: Protesters Clash With Police in Turkey
2013-12-28.abstract: Thousands of protesters in Istanbul called for the resignation of Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan over a corruption scandal.

2013-12-28.title: Projects Under Scrutiny Displace Istanbul’s Poor
2013-12-28.abstract: Development projects like the ones at the center of a widening corruption investigation in Turkey have left migrants from the countryside wondering where they will go.

2013-12-28.title: Holocaust History, as Told by a Survivor
2013-12-28.abstract: Laszlo Schwartz, once mostly silent about his harrowing childhood as a prisoner of the Nazis, now spends much of his time telling his story in German classrooms.

2013-12-28.title: First Attempts Fail to Free Ship Stranded by Heavy Ice
2013-12-28.abstract: Icebreakers sent to free a stranded Russian research ship, the Akademik Shokalskiy, off Antarctica were stopped by heavy ice within sight of the ship, officials said early Saturday.

2013-12-28.title: Libya Holds Four American Military Personnel for Hours
2013-12-28.abstract: The four, who were believed to have been reviewing evacuation routes for diplomats, were released after being held for hours at the Interior Ministry.

2013-12-29.title: Deadly Shootout and Arrest in Iraq Set Off Sunni Protests
2013-12-29.abstract: A raid by Iraqi security forces on the home of a prominent Sunni member of Parliament on Saturday morning in Anbar Province set off a two-hour gun battle that left the lawmaker’s brother and five guards dead, along with a soldier, Iraqi security and medical officials said.

2013-12-29.title: Deadly Syrian Bomb Strikes Crowded Aleppo Market
2013-12-29.abstract: At least 21 people were killed at a market as government forces kept up a bombing campaign.

2013-12-29.title: New Law All but Bars Russian GPS Sites in U.S.
2013-12-29.abstract: A measure that was signed into law on Thursday virtually bars Moscow from building monitor stations on American soil that critics feared could be used for snooping or worse.

2013-12-29.title: Russia Screening of Pussy Riot Film Is Blocked
2013-12-29.abstract: Directors of a state-financed theater in Moscow received a call from authorities threatening their jobs if they screened a documentary about the activist group.

2013-12-29.title: Polling Comes to Afghanistan, Suggesting Limit to Sway of President Karzai
2013-12-29.abstract: Three recent polls are giving Afghans a crash course in front-runners, horse-race coverage and candidates who eagerly dismiss any numbers that do not put them out front, topics familiar to Americans.

2013-12-30.title: Second Icebreaker Nears Ship Stranded Off Antarctica
2013-12-30.abstract: Strong winds last Tuesday packed loose ice around the ship, which is carrying researchers and tourists.

2013-12-30.title: Brazil Forging Economic Ties With Cuba, While Hiring Its Doctors
2013-12-30.abstract: Brazil is vying to exert influence in Cuba as the authorities in Havana slowly expose the island nation’s economy to market forces.

2013-12-30.title: Funeral Turnout Shows Lebanon’s Ebbing Morale
2013-12-30.abstract: In a sign of how political passions have waned since mass protests helped drive out the Syrians in 2005, barely 1,000 people attended the funeral of Mohamad B. Chatah, killed by a car bomb in Beirut on Friday.

2013-12-30.title: In South Sudan Camp, Lives Compressed
2013-12-30.abstract: As many as 22,000 people from around the world crammed into a United Nations peacekeeping base outside Malakal, South Sudan, are bound together by hunger, thirst and fear.

2013-12-30.title: Vito Rizzuto, Reputed Mafia Boss of Canada, Dies at 67
2013-12-30.abstract: Mr. Rizzuto’s dapper outfits and his ability to avoid prison led the authorities to call him the John Gotti of Montreal.

2013-12-31.title: Clothing Brands Sidestep Blame for Safety Lapses
2013-12-31.abstract: Months after the collapse of the Rana Plaza complex in Bangladesh exposed abuses in the garment industry, there is no consensus on what responsibility global chains should bear for reform and compensation.

2013-12-31.title: China: Police Kill 8 Assailants in a Clash in Xinjiang
2013-12-31.abstract: Violence between civilians and security forces has become increasingly common in Xinjiang, where officials say they are trying to deal with rebels or terrorists.

2013-12-31.title: Swiss Give Visa to Freed Russian Oil Tycoon
2013-12-31.abstract: Switzerland granted Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky a three-month visa entitling him to visit the country where his wife has reportedly lived while he was in prison.

2013-12-31.title: Lebanon, Worried About Border, Fires on Syrian Helicopters in Its Airspace
2013-12-31.abstract: The army’s move was the first such action since a threat last summer to attack troops, vehicles or warplanes entering its territory from Syria.

2013-12-31.title: Beyond Camps, Aiding Syrians Is Even Harder
2013-12-31.abstract: Aid groups have strained to help the more than 1.8 million registered Syrian refugees dispersed in Lebanon, Jordan, Turkey and elsewhere, who are harder to reach than those in camps.
